activities_projects.title: Spotting the Funny Man
activities_projects.objective: Identify the physical traits of the character in the poem
steps: Read the poem 'The Funny Man' carefully
Find three physical features mentioned (e.g., his face, his eyes, or his clothes)
Write these features down in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice identifying descriptive details in poetry
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of physical traits (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Rhyme Hunt
steps: Look at the end of each line in the first two stanzas
Find three pairs of words that sound the same (e.g., 'man' and 'can')
List them in your notebook as 'Rhyming Pairs'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the basic structure of rhyming verse

activities_projects.title: Drawing the Funny Man
activities_projects.objective: Visualize a character based on a text
steps: Think about the funny things the man does in the poem
Draw a picture of him performing one of these actions
Use bright colors to make him look as 'funny' as possible
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students translate verbal descriptions into visual art
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to the poem's description (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Making Faces
activities_projects.objective: Express emotions through facial expressions like the character
steps: The funny man makes people laugh with his expressions
Try to make a 'funny face' in front of a mirror or a friend
Write one word to describe the feeling you wanted to show (e.g., silly, surprised)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students explore non-verbal communication and mood
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and descriptive word choice (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: Happy Words
steps: Find words in the poem that mean someone is laughing or happy
Think of two more words on your own (e.g., 'jolly', 'silly')
Write a sentence using one of these words
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct word usage in a sentence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Laughing Stanza
steps: Pick your favorite stanza from the poem
Read it aloud to your partner
Try to use a 'funny' voice to make your partner smile
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve their reading fluency and tone
activities_projects.evaluation: Expression and clarity during recitation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Action Verbs
steps: Look for words that show the funny man moving (e.g., 'jump', 'spin', 'grin')
List 4 action verbs found in the text
Act out one of the verbs for the class to guess
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize verbs in a literary context

activities_projects.title: Why is it Funny?
activities_projects.objective: Understand the cause of humor in the poem
steps: Think about what the funny man does that makes people laugh
Write 2 sentences explaining why the children in the poem like him
Start with: 'The children laugh because...'
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation based on the text (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: A New Hat for the Funny Man
activities_projects.objective: Use imagination to add to a character
steps: The poem describes the man's outfit. Imagine he gets a new, silly hat
Draw the hat and write 2 adjectives to describe it (e.g., 'tall', 'spotted')
Share your design with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice using adjectives and creative thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and correct use of adjectives (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Funny Man’s Message
activities_projects.objective: Summarize the theme of spreading joy
steps: Does the funny man make people feel good or bad?
Write a 'Thank You' note to the funny man in 3 short sentences
Tell him why it is important to laugh
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students reflect on the positive social impact of humor
activities_projects.evaluation: Tone of the note and clarity of thought (10 marks)
